data.title: Wall Street Breakfast Podcast: Microsoft, OpenAI Chart New Path
data.description:
Listen below or on the go on Apple Podcasts and Spotify

Microsoft, OpenAI (MSFT) redefine ties in shift to for-profit model. (00:24) Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) jumps on report Paramount Skydance (PSKY) preparing offer. (01:37) Apple Watch (AAPL) hypertension detection to roll out next week after FDA nod- report. (02:56)

Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and OpenAI have signed a non-binding memorandum of understanding to redefine their relationship, paving the way for OpenAI to restructure into a for-profit company.

According to an official statement, “OpenAI’s planned evolution will see the existing OpenAI nonprofit both control a Public Benefit Corporation (PBC) and share directly in its success. OpenAI started as a nonprofit, remains one today, and will continue to be one—with the nonprofit holding the authority that guides our future.”

OpenAI’s nonprofit arm stands to receive over $100 billion, or about 20% of the $500 billion valuation the company is targeting in private markets.

Microsoft and OpenAI are working toward a final agreement.

Microsoft (MSFT) has been a key OpenAI backer, investing $1 billion in 2019 and $10 billion in 2023. The earlier deal gave Microsoft exclusive rights to sell OpenAI tools through Azure and have preferred access to its technology.

This year, however, OpenAI began diversifying by building its own data center project, “Stargate,” and signing major cloud deals with Oracle (ORCL) and Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) worth more than $300 billion.

The restructuring still requires approval from regulators in California and Delaware.

Warner Bros. Discovery (NASDAQ:WBD) jumped 29% on Thursday after a report that Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) is preparing a majority cash bid. Paramount Skydance climbed 15.5%.

According to a WSJ report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter, the offer will be for the entire company, including its cable networks and movie studio. The bid will be backed by the Ellison family.

According to CNBC's David Faber, a Paramount Skydance bid for Warner Bros. Discovery may come as soon as next week.

The potential bid comes after Warner Bros. Discovery (NASDAQ:WBD) in June confirmed that it would separate into two companies, with one focused on streaming and the other on its television networks.

A deal would combine Paramount's CBS News with Warner Bros. CNN as well as Warner Bros. studio with Paramount studio.

Paramount Skydance may be trying to make a bid before Warner Bros.' planned split in an attempt to pre-empt a possible bidding war for the studio and streaming unit that could include Amazon.com (AMZN) and Apple (AAPL), according to the WSJ report.

A Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) bid would also follow after Oracle Corp. co-founder Larry Ellison's wealth jumped by more than $100 billion on Wednesday after the stock 36% surge, at one point making him the richest person in the world, over taking Elon Musk, according to the Bloomberg's billionaire index.

Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) gets the go ahead from U.S. Food and Drug Administration for its new hypertension detection tool for Apple Watch. It'll roll out next week.

The tool uses data from the Apple Watch’s optical heart sensor to analyze how a user’s blood vessels respond to heartbeats over a 30-day time period, Apple reported.

The hypertension detection tool has been developed using machine learning trained on data from studies with over 100,000 participants and is compatible with Apple Watch Series 9, 10, 11, Ultra 2, and Ultra 3.

The tool will notify the smartwatch user if any signs of chronic hypertension are detected and would encourage sharing results with a medical provider.

The feature will be available in 150 regions, including the US, EU, and Hong Kong.

Apple unveiled its new Apple Watch Series 11, Ultra 3, and SE models at its latest product launch event earlier this week.

What’s Trending on Seeking Alpha:

United CEO sees Spirit Airlines going out of business - report

Tesla engineer quits after 8 years, criticizes Elon Musk's leadership in exit letter

Nvidia's supplier SK hynix ready with next-gen memory chip for AI

Catalyst watch:

  • Take-Two Interactive (TTWO) will release the Borderlands 4 videogame for PlayStation 5, Windows, and Xbox Series X/S, with a Nintendo Switch 2 release following on October 3.

Dow, S&P and Nasdaq futures are in the red. Crude oil is up 0.2% at $62/barrel. Bitcoin is down 0.3% at $115,000. Gold is up 0.2% at $3,638.

The FTSE 100 is up 0.2% and the DAX is down 0.3%.

The biggest movers for the day premarket: RH (NYSE:RH) -7% - Shares sank after the upscale furniture retailer missed Q2 expectations and cut its full-year forecast, citing new U.S. tariffs, delayed catalog releases, and a weak housing market.

On today’s economic calendar:

  • 10:00 am Consumer Sentiment

And finally, our most recent news quiz just dropped. If you haven’t checked it out, it’s 10 questions all about the news of the week. One of the questions this week is about a story we talked about today on Wall Street Breakfast but it has a twist. It says: Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) soared on a report that Paramount Skydance (PSKY) is preparing an all-cash bid for the company. If the deal goes through, which planet will the combined company NOT have the rights to?

I can’t wait to see if you get it right. I’ll leave a link to the quiz in the show notes section.

data.title: BP: Strategic Realignment And Cost-Saving Initiatives May Expand Profitability And Cash Flow Margins
data.description:
Introduction

Based on my analysis, BP p.l.c. (NYSE:BP) presents an attractive investment opportunity for investors who are looking to gain exposure to the energy sector. Apart from the BP's strategic realignment, the company is also committed to improving its profitability by further optimizing its operations and cost structure. Additionally, if BP can maintain its current performance, there is no doubt that shareholders will continue to see attractive returns in the form of repurchases and dividends. Based on my valuation analysis, BP presents a potential upside of more than 40%. Hence, in this report, I will demonstrate why investors should consider BP in their portfolio.

Company Overview

BP is one of the world's most renowned energy companies, based in the United Kingdom. Incorporated in 1908, BP now has more than 100.5k full-time employees around the world, and the company's headquarters is currently in London, United Kingdom.

Based on the company's filings, BP's operations are segregated into the following segments: (1) Gas & Low Carbon Energy, (2) Oil Production & Operations, (3) Customers & Products, (4) Other Businesses & Corporate Activities. Most of BP's sales are derived from Gas & Low Carbon Energy and Customers & Product Segments; these account for 18.9% and 80.4%, respectively. It is important to note that these are revenues after eliminating sales between segments. Separately, close to 66% of BP's revenues are derived outside the United States.

Latest Developments

In the most recent quarter, BP generated $46.62 billion, representing a decline of -1.4% y/y. In 2Q25, BP also saw deteriorated topline performance in most of its core segments. With the exception of BP's Gas & Low Carbon Energy segment, BP's individual segments saw declines of between -8.7% to -65.3%.

During this period, profitability improved primarily due to lower expenses. As a percentage of total revenues, total purchases fell to 57.64% from 61.08% in the same period last year, whereas production and manufacturing expenses fell to 13.2% as compared to 14.15% in the same period last year. Net income grew more than 26.5x as compared to the same period last year, while net income margin expanded by 400 bps to 4.14% as compared to 0.15% in the same period last year.

Persisting Demand For Black Gold Supports BP's Strategic Realignment On Traditional Sources Of Energy

Even though the world has invested heavily in alternative energy in recent years, multiple indicators continue to suggest that demand for traditional oil and gas remains surprisingly high. In a recent study conducted by OIES, oil demand is unlikely to peak anytime soon, and global oil demand is expected to expand by 6.2 million barrels per day to hit 108 mb/d by FY2030 from 101.8 mb/d in FY2023.

The primary drivers identified by OIES for the continued expansion are: (1) increasing non-OECD demand and (2) persisting growth in petrochemicals, jet fuel, and kerosene. Current studies on gas also revealed a similar trend. According to IGU, demand for gas will continue to trek towards the upside primarily due to support from non-OECD countries.

More importantly, BP recognizes that traditional sources of energy are here to stay and has already started to strategically reposition itself; for now, BP aims to generate higher returns from oil and gas instead of investing aggressively in renewables, which generally have lesser returns in the short term. This is also because the company realized that it has underperformed its peers that are less aggressive in renewables.

As such, in the foreseeable future, BP will reinvest primarily in traditional oil and gas; investment into renewables is estimated to be less than 5% of the company's total capital expenditures. Thus far, for FY2025, BP already has 10 exploration discoveries in multiple countries such as Brazil, India, and Egypt and another 5 new oil and gas projects in Angola, United States, Trinidad, and Tobago. Pertaining to BP's renewables portfolio, the company will be divesting many of its less efficient operations, such as its US onshore wind and Australian Renewable Energy Hub.

Profitability and Cash Flow Margins May Further Expand Due to Cost Reduction Initiatives

Apart from the company's strategic realignment, the company continues to make progress in its cost reduction program. As of 2Q25, BP has already delivered $1.7 billion out of its $4 billion OPEX reduction target; more than 75% of the achieved reductions are due to supply chain optimization and organizational transformation.

By the end of 2025, BP will further streamline its operations, eliminating another 6.2k full-time employees. Additionally, BP will continue to invest in artificial intelligence and automation to further reduce its overall costs; recently, BP utilized generative AI for its Azerbaijan operations, reducing the total time for well planning by 90%. Separately, BP will work with Palantir to further optimize its refinery operations.

As a reference, the $4 billion reduction represents approximately 2.14% of the company's annualized revenue. More importantly, if the company is able to maintain its current traction, BP's profit and cash flow margins can potentially further expand. In fact, BP's profitability is already improving; the company's most recent net income margin expanded by more than 400 bps as compared to the same period last year. From an FCF perspective, BP believes that the company's FCF will be able to grow at a CAGR of 20% per annum till FY2027.

BP Continues To Return Value to Shareholders Despite Ongoing Realignment

It is important to note that BP has been consistently returning value to its shareholders in recent years. For the past ten years, BP has returned $87.15 billion to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends. As a reference, $87.15 billion is more than 95% of the company's current market capitalization.

Looking ahead, shareholders should expect to see BP continue returning its excess cash through both share repurchases and dividends. In fact, BP recently announced a $750 million share buyback and a 4% increase in dividends. Therefore, as long as BP maintains its current traction and progress, it is reasonable for investors to expect BP to continue returning value through share repurchases and dividends.

Valuation Analysis Implies An Upside Potential Of More Than 40%

From a relative valuation perspective, BP can be considered slightly more attractive as compared to its peers. Currently, BP is trading at a P/OCF and EV/EBITDA of 3.75x and 5.4x as compared to the industry median of 5.26x and 6.84x. That being said, it is important to note that the company's forward P/E is currently trading at 15.09x which is above the industry median of 13.24x.

To ascertain the valuation of BP, I have used the following assumptions and variables:

  • Forward P/E to remain at 15.09x. This multiple is based on BP's current forward P/E. Given that BP has multiple upcoming positive catalysts due to its cost reduction initiatives and strategic repositioning, I believe that BP will not see any P/E multiple contraction.
  • FY2027 EPS of $3.26 per share. This is based on current consensus estimates. FY2027 EPS is chosen to reflect the potential success of the company's cost reduction initiatives and strategic repositioning. Additionally, it is likely that BP's profit level will start to normalize by FY2027.

Based on these assumptions and variables, BP's share price should be worth $49.19 per share by the end of FY2027. Currently, BP trades at $34.76, implying a potential upside of 41.51%.

Closing Remarks & Investment Risks

Overall, BP presents an attractive opportunity for investors who are looking to gain exposure in the energy sector. If BP's strategic realignment succeeds, there is a strong possibility that the company will be able to improve its profitability and cash flow metrics.

Moreover, BP is not only just focusing on the top line. The company's cost-saving initiatives through optimization and experimentation of new technologies like artificial intelligence will further support its quest for higher returns. More importantly, BP is currently cheap and offers a potential upside of more than 40%.

That being said, we are currently in a period of great macroeconomic uncertainty; there are many indicators that suggest that the deterioration of the economy is far from over. If macroeconomic conditions worsen, there is a strong likelihood that oil prices may plunge. Unfortunately, BP's ability to improve its cash flow and profitability metrics is highly dependent on oil prices. Therefore, investors who are interested in BP should closely monitor the global macroeconomy and oil prices. If oil prices further capitulate, it will render my thesis invalid.
